system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

Conversation started: Saturday, April 25, 2026 03:56 AM
Model: deepseek-chat
Provider: deepseek

You are on a text messaging communication platform, WhatsApp. Please do not use markdown as it does not render. You can send media files natively: to deliver a file to the user, include MEDIA:/absolute/path/to/file in your response. The file will be sent as a native WhatsApp attachment — images (.jpg, .png, .webp) appear as photos, videos (.mp4, .mov) play inline, and other files arrive as downloadable documents. You can also include image URLs in markdown format ![alt](url) and they will be sent as photos.

function.name: vision_analyze

image_url.description: Image URL (http/https) or local file path to analyze.

question.description: Your specific question or request about the image to resolve. The AI will automatically provide a complete image description AND answer your specific question.

messages.content:
The API consistently rejects the image. The error "unknown variant `image_url`, expected `text`" indicates this particular API endpoint doesn't support `image_url` in the messages array - it only accepts text. Since I have no other tools available (no bash, no file manipulation), I cannot proceed with the image analysis.

Let me check if there's perhaps an alternative way to reference the image or if there's other context I can use.

Given the constraints, I cannot directly analyze either image file (`/root/.hermes/image_cache/img_bc480d4ddb8c.jpg` or `/tmp/g1_april2026.png`) because the `vision_analyze` API endpoint does not support image URLs in the format being sent - it expects text-only messages and the `image_url` variant is not recognized by this provider's endpoint.

**Summary:**
- **What I did:** Attempted to use `vision_analyze` three times with both available image paths (`/root/.hermes/image_cache/img_bc480d4ddb8c.jpg` and `/tmp/g1_april2026.png`).
- **What I found/accomplished:** The vision API consistently returns error code 400 with "unknown variant `image_url`, expected `text`", indicating the API endpoint this agent uses does not support image_url-based vision analysis — it only accepts text messages.
- **Files created/modified:** None.
- **Issues encountered:** The `vision_analyze` tool's underlying API provider (DeepSeek) does not appear to support the `image_url` content type in messages. Only text content is accepted. Without alternative tools (bash, Python/PIL, curl, etc.) I cannot convert, resize, or re-encode the image, nor can I use a different API endpoint. The task cannot be completed with the available tooling.
